failure_signature.details: The model 'gpt-4-turbo' does not exist. Did you mean 'gpt-4-turbo-2024-04-09'? Available models can be listed via the /v1/models endpoint.

problem.goal_state: Initialize LangChain agent with specific GPT-4 model variant

solutions.solution_summary: Use the correct model identifier and implement a model alias mapping
solution_steps: Check available models via the API: curl https://api.openai.com/v1/models -H 'Authorization: Bearer $OPENAI_API_KEY' | jq '.data[].id'
Use the canonical model name: replace 'gpt-4-turbo' with 'gpt-4-turbo-2024-04-09' or the current alias 'gpt-4-turbo'
Create a model alias configuration that maps friendly names to exact model IDs for easy updates
Subscribe to OpenAI's model deprecation announcements to stay ahead of model name changes
Implement a fallback chain: try the preferred model first, then fall back to alternatives

failure_signature.details: The response was filtered due to the prompt triggering Azure OpenAI's content management policy. Reason: violence. Policy: Azure default content filter.

problem.goal_state: AutoGen agent analyzing security incident reports containing threat descriptions

solutions.solution_summary: Adjust content filter settings and restructure prompts to avoid false positives
solution_steps: If using Azure OpenAI, request a content filter exception through the Azure portal for your specific use case
Restructure the prompt to use clinical and technical language instead of graphic descriptions
Add a system message preamble explaining the professional security analysis context: 'You are analyzing cybersecurity incident reports in a professional security operations context.'
Pre-process input data to redact or sanitize content that may trigger filters before sending to the LLM
Consider using the direct OpenAI API instead of Azure OpenAI if content filters are too restrictive for your use case

failure_signature.details: The model 'gpt-4-0613' has been deprecated as of 2025-06-06. Please migrate to 'gpt-4o' or a newer model. This model will stop working after 2025-09-06.

problem.goal_state: Migrate production CrewAI pipeline from deprecated model

solutions.solution_summary: Systematically migrate to the recommended replacement model with regression testing
solution_steps: Identify the recommended replacement model from OpenAI's deprecation guide: gpt-4-0613 should be replaced with gpt-4o
Update the model configuration in CrewAI: set llm=ChatOpenAI(model='gpt-4o') on all Agent definitions
Run your existing test suite and evaluation benchmarks against the new model to detect any behavioral regressions
Pay special attention to output format changes: gpt-4o may format structured outputs differently than gpt-4-0613
Update any model-specific prompt engineering (e.g., token limits, temperature settings) to match the new model's characteristics
Deploy the migration incrementally: route 10% of traffic to the new model initially and monitor error rates

failure_signature.details: Response was blocked by Gemini safety filters. Category: HARM_CATEGORY_DANGEROUS_CONTENT. Threshold: BLOCK_MEDIUM_AND_ABOVE. Probability: MEDIUM.

problem.goal_state: LangChain agent generating penetration testing commands for security audit

solutions.solution_summary: Adjust Gemini safety settings for the specific professional use case
solution_steps: Configure safety settings on the model to lower the threshold for the relevant category: safety_settings={HarmCategory.HARM_CATEGORY_DANGEROUS_CONTENT: HarmBlockThreshold.BLOCK_ONLY_HIGH}
When using LangChain, pass safety settings through model_kwargs: ChatGoogleGenerativeAI(model='gemini-1.5-pro', safety_settings={...})
Frame the request in a professional context: add system instructions that establish the authorized security testing scenario
If safety filters remain too restrictive, consider switching to a model with configurable content policies for enterprise security use cases
Implement a prompt rewriting layer that uses technical terminology and avoids trigger phrases while preserving semantic meaning

failure_signature.details: Overloaded: Anthropic's API is temporarily overloaded. Please retry your request after a brief wait.

problem.goal_state: Production agent handling concurrent user requests via Anthropic API

solutions.solution_summary: Implement retry with backoff and add a model failover strategy
solution_steps: Add exponential backoff retry specifically for overloaded_error: start at 5 seconds, max 60 seconds, with 3-5 retries
Implement a model failover: if claude-3.5-sonnet is overloaded, automatically switch to claude-3-haiku or gpt-4o-mini as a fallback
Monitor Anthropic's status page at status.anthropic.com and implement a circuit breaker that activates during known outages
Add request queuing with a max concurrency limit to reduce the chance of contributing to overload conditions
Consider using Anthropic's batch API for non-time-sensitive requests to reduce pressure on the real-time API
